transcript.content: I want to build a multiplayer party game app.
Cool! What kind of game mechanics are you thinking?
It's like a trivia or drawing game. One player creates a game room and others join with a code.
Got it - so we need game creation and joining. How does that work?
When someone creates a game, they enter their name like 'Stan' and get a game code. Then they wait in a lobby.
Other players enter the game code in a PIN input field, then enter their name like 'John', and they join the same lobby.
Should joining happen in the same browser tab?
No, it should be in a new tab - like a second player on the same computer for testing.
Perfect. So we have create game and join game flows. Anything else?
That's the core flow for now. Players should be able to see each other in the lobby once joined.
Alright, I'll build the game creation and joining with lobby visibility. Let's start!

steps: Navigate to the application URL (usually http://localhost:3000 or similar)

ux_tasks.requirement: The user must be able to create a new game room
steps: Locate the 'Create Game' button or link.
Click the 'Create Game' button or link.
Locate the 'Enter Your Name' input field.
Type 'Stan' into the 'Enter Your Name' field.
Click the 'Create' or 'Submit' button.
ux_tasks.acceptance_criteria: A game code is displayed. A lobby screen appears with 'Stan' listed as a player.

ux_tasks.requirement: The user must be able to join an existing game room in a new tab
steps: Open a new browser tab.
Navigate to the application URL (usually http://localhost:3000 or similar) in the new tab.
Locate the 'Join Game' button or link.
Click the 'Join Game' button or link.
Locate the 'Enter Game Code' or 'PIN' input field.
Type the game code generated in Task 1 into the 'Enter Game Code' field.
Locate the 'Enter Your Name' input field.
Type 'John' into the 'Enter Your Name' field.
Click the 'Join' or 'Submit' button.
ux_tasks.acceptance_criteria: The new tab displays the same lobby as in Task 1. Both 'Stan' and 'John' are listed as players in the lobby in both tabs.

steps: Navigate to https://zv997r-8000.csb.app/

agent2_input.requirement: The user must be able to create a new game room
steps: Locate the 'Create Game' button or link.
Click the 'Create Game' button or link.
Locate the 'Enter Your Name' input field.
Type 'Stan' into the 'Enter Your Name' field.
Click the 'Create' or 'Submit' button.
agent2_input.acceptance_criteria: A game code is displayed. A lobby screen appears with 'Stan' listed as a player.

agent2_input.requirement: The user must be able to join an existing game room in a new tab
steps: Open a new browser tab.
Navigate to the application URL (usually http://localhost:3000 or similar) in the new tab.
Locate the 'Join Game' button or link.
Click the 'Join Game' button or link.
Locate the 'Enter Game Code' or 'PIN' input field.
Type the game code generated in Task 1 into the 'Enter Game Code' field.
Locate the 'Enter Your Name' input field.
Type 'John' into the 'Enter Your Name' field.
Click the 'Join' or 'Submit' button.
agent2_input.acceptance_criteria: The new tab displays the same lobby as in Task 1. Both 'Stan' and 'John' are listed as players in the lobby in both tabs.

task_results.requirement: The user must be able to create a new game room
task_results.observations: Successfully created game room. Used multi-action sequence for name input and creation button click.

task_results.requirement: The user must be able to join an existing game room in a new tab
task_results.observations: Successfully joined the game in a new tab. Had to re-evaluate indexes for the 4-digit input fields (81-84) after initial attempt. The 'Join Game' button was successfully clicked (index 85). Both tabs correctly displayed both players ('Stan' and 'John').

task_results.summary: Successfully executed two UX tasks: creating a game room (Task 1) and joining it in a new tab (Task 2). All acceptance criteria were met. The application handled multi-tab state synchronization correctly.
